prompt:
The hiring coordinator is checking two candidate records before the recruiter handoff. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Audit the candidate named “Avery Johnson - Fleet Coordinator” at “Operations” and the candidate named “Samira Patel - Benefits Specialist” at “People Operations” using the genuine `candidatectl` executable provided in this workspace. Its local manual is `CANDIDATECTL.md`; you may inspect the manual and run the failing verification before beginning candidate-record operations. Do not inspect the backing data files directly.

The first candidate-record action must launch both exact name-and-location searches concurrently in one shell-tool action. Wait for both results. A branch becomes eligible for retrieval only when its own search returns exactly one stable ID; never infer an ID from the prompt, another branch, or repository contents. When both branches are eligible, the next candidate-record action must launch both ID retrievals concurrently in one shell-tool action. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from successfully retrieved full records, never from search results.

This is a read-only audit. Do not use any collection-wide, saved-preference, availability, creation, update, cancellation, or notification operation. Do not modify `candidatectl`, `CANDIDATECTL.md`, anything under `data/` or `tests/`, or the executable-created `.candidate-audit/journal.jsonl`; the journal must be produced solely by real `candidatectl` processes.

Write the audit to `candidate_audit.txt`. For each resolved branch, in the order requested above, write exactly one line in this format: `<name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <date> | <status>`. Follow those record lines with exactly one sentence in this format: `Status comparison: The statuses are <the same|different>: <first status> versus <second status>.` Do not put headings, commentary, unresolved guesses, distractors, or any other fields in that file. Run `python3 tests/verify_audit.py` when finished.
